comment: Three dosing regimens compared in comp-036 to find the minimum-N-doses that achieves the receptor-occupancy bar over the 72h gout flare window.
comment_on_simulation_hours: 168 hours = 7 days. Captures the full flare window plus run-off after dosing stops, so trailing occupancy decline is visible in plots.

A_qd.name: Once-daily (QD)
A_qd.comment: Simplest patient adherence. Each administration is one nebulizer session. Find smallest N where >=80% sustained occupancy over 0-72h holds at median and at p25.

B_bid.name: Twice-daily (BID)
B_bid.comment: Better trough coverage. Common in acute infectious/inflammatory therapy. Find smallest N (= N_days x 2) where >=80% sustained occupancy over 0-72h holds.

C_loading_plus_maintenance.name: Loading dose + QD maintenance
C_loading_plus_maintenance.comment: Double-dose at hour 0, then standard maintenance doses every 24h. Loading-dose strategy commonly used to reach steady state faster; particularly relevant for acute therapy where the first 0-24h matters most. Total mRNA exposure = (1 + N_maintenance) x base dose for cost/safety accounting; the '2x loading' multiplier is on dose not on inhaler-session-count.

comment_on_dose_per_administration: Central dose used for the regimen-comparison simulations. Within the comp-033 range (4-24 mg). Each Monte Carlo iteration samples a dose from log-uniform [4, 24] mg; the regimen-comparison ranking is robust across this range because superposition holds.
comment_on_lung_irritation: Soft constraint, not a hard gate. Inhaled-LNP human tolerance data (Translate Bio MRT5005 CF trial reported transient cough in 30-40% of patients; ReCode RCT2100 similar) suggests >4x daily nebulizer sessions is impractical at home. QID (every 6h) and Q3H regimens are mechanically possible but not patient-feasible.
comment_on_max_days: Hard upper bound for acute-flare therapy. Anakinra (off-label) typically 3-5 days. Saag 2021 RCT used 5 days. Pred taper is 7-10 days inclusive of taper. Anything >14 days starts looking like chronic therapy not acute-flare-abort, which is a different value proposition.

decision_rule_for_each_regimen.GREEN_per_regimen: median occupancy >= 80% sustained over 0-72h flare window AND p25 occupancy >= 60% (confidence bounds clear of catastrophic failure) AND dose count and frequency are clinically practical (<=14 days, <=4 doses/day)
decision_rule_for_each_regimen.YELLOW_per_regimen: median occupancy 60-80% sustained OR median >=80% only achievable at >14 days OR >4 doses/day
decision_rule_for_each_regimen.RED_per_regimen: median occupancy <60% sustained at maximum-practical regimen
